entries.title: [CCSYNC-01] Agent Status must stop showing stale lane/read-model rows as attention-required terminals
entries.notes: CCSYNC-01 (PAR-226) is NOT done. It asks Agent Status to validate receipt paths and live pane/process evidence before classifying a row as attention-required, so rows with review.state=pending but a missing pending_review_path render as stale projection / needs reconciliation rather than counting in the attention/action badge. Current code does the opposite: getNodeStatusGroup (tree provider ~5112-5134) routes pending-review + missing-receipt rows to 'attention'->'action' (badge-counted), and the existing test agent-status-review-queue-gap.test.ts:242 explicitly asserts that. No CCSYNC-01 marker, closeout doc, or regression fixture exists; WORK-LEDGER.md:24 lists it as an open P0. The related PAR-195 (completed detached Symphony lanes) is independently satisfied (commit 515e132, agent-status-symphony-detached.test.ts), and the 2026-06-16 receipt-truth/reviewer-lane hardening is adjacent prior art but does not implement this issue.

entries.notes: CP-33 is a confirmed, still-open bug. classifyTimePeriod derives yesterday/last7/last30 boundaries by subtracting fixed 24h multiples from local midnight, so on DST transition days (23h/25h offsets) timestamps near midnight land in the wrong bucket. entries.title: [CP-07] Failed extraction leaves the existing Ghostty install renamed as the backup
entries.notes: [CP-07] data-loss bug is still live and unfixed. BinaryManager.downloadRelease renames the existing Ghostty.app to .bak (line 271) before calling extractZip (line 275) with no surrounding try/catch. If extractZip throws (corrupt zip, missing unzip, timeout, interrupt), the method exits leaving the user with no Ghostty.app — the backup is only ever restored when post-extraction validation returns false, not when extraction itself fails. No fix commit, no closeout doc, and no BinaryManager test file exist. entries.title: [CP-09] Windows repository matching uses POSIX separators
entries.notes: [CP-09] is unfixed. findRepositoryForFile in src/utils/git-repo-utils.ts still uses POSIX `${path}/` string-prefix containment in both the direct (L40/L49) and nested-repo (L64/L68/L78) branches, plus the realpath sub-branches, so on Windows (backslash fsPath) only the exact-root case matches and ordinary files inside a repo return undefined. entries.title: [CP-05] Windows child files cannot be mapped to their workspace provider
entries.notes: [CP-05] / PAR-48 is NOT done. The described bug is still present verbatim in src/factories/provider-factory.ts:168-172: getProviderForFile builds workspaceWithSep with a hardcoded '/' and does filePath.startsWith(...), so on Windows where fsPath uses backslashes (C:\\repo\\src\\a.ts vs prefix C:\\repo/) nested files fail to resolve and only the exact-root match works. No platform-aware normalization, no Node path import, no Windows regression test exists, and the work ledger lists it unchecked. Fix needs path-aware containment in getProviderForFile plus a unit test covering POSIX and Windows separators; coordinate with PAR-51/[CP-09] which is the analogous POSIX-separator bug.

entries.notes: [CP-28] bug is still fully present and unaddressed.
